unnecessary_complexity.searchPrompt: Only match if the staged change shows a concrete, local, developer-actionable warning. Do not match because a helper, module, type, or exported function exists. Do not match normal feature structure. Do not match prompt strings, docs, domain constants, configuration, generated text, or test fixtures unless the enabled pattern explicitly concerns them. A match should make the developer think: "Yes, this is exactly the kind of thing AI might have talked me into." If the warning would be embarrassing to show in a pre-commit hook, omit it.
matchExamples: A two-line inline operation becomes a new service/helper with one call site and the decision is harder to see.
nonMatchExamples: A helper used in multiple places.
A real feature module with coherent ownership.
A boundary around an external dependency, API, auth, or framework concern.
